items.title: Russia’s Amazon-style retail giant Wildberries is now in Ukraine’s crosshairs. Here's why
items.description: Ukraine has ratcheted up its attacks on Russia's Amazon-style retail giant Wildberries, signaling a new phase in the war.

items.title: U.S. oil tops $90, Brent above $98 after tankers struck off Saudi Arabia
items.description: Oil prices rose after attacks on ships in the Red Sea, and U.S. President Donald Trump's threatened to bomb Iranian infrastructure.

items.title: UniCredit CEO tells CNBC acquisition of Commerzbank could happen in fourth quarter
items.description: UniCredit CEO Andrea Orcel told CNBC Thursday that the Italian bank could "potentially" acquire Commerzbank in the fourth quarter.

items.title: Houthis claim strikes on Saudi Arabian oil tankers as Rubio says Iran not ready to make a deal
items.description: Yemen's Houthis said they had targeted two Saudi Arabian oil tankers with drones and missiles for allegedly violating its maritime blockade.

items.title: Hedge funds circle UK stocks as new PM Andy Burnham pledges 'new economic model'
items.description: Prime Minister Andy Burnham's overhaul of Britain's domestic policy could fuel a range of trading opportunities in U.K. stocks.

items.title: Silver and gold prices stage a cautious rebound — but analysts see slim hopes for a sustained rally
items.description: Precious metals have rallied in recent days, but analysts say prices could face a difficult path back to all-time highs achieved earlier this year.

items.title: Gen Z protests led by India’s 'Cockroach party' are testing Modi’s leadership
items.description: Student protests organized by a pseudo-political outfit, Cockroach Janta Party, are gaining momentum as they challenge the political establishment.

items.title: Chip firm priced as China's most valuable company before its IPO brings scrutiny to crypto platform
items.description: The outsized premium was due in part to offshore investors who, locked out of the highly anticipated listings, turned to crypto for a parallel market.

items.title: Inside India newsletter: Skyroot rocket puts India alongside U.S., China in private orbital launch
items.description: Indian startup Skyroot joins Chinese and American private companies in the race to launch satellites in low orbit.

items.title: Alphabet and Tesla test Wall Street's patience as AI spending overshadows growth
items.description: Shares of Alphabet and Tesla dipped after the companies reported massive spending increases in their quarterly earnings reports.

items.title: Why we're sticking with Alphabet despite an imperfect quarter and more AI spending
items.description: The market isn't in a forgiving mood, but we're not in a hurry to leave this stock behind.

items.title: IBM lowers full-year forecast after earnings warning
items.description: IBM is now looking to improve productivity with artificial intelligence, including with a new coding tool called Bob.

items.title: Southwest Airlines' third-quarter forecast falls short as fuel bill climbs
items.description: Southwest Airlines reported a more than 9% increase in second-quarter profit as higher fares are increasingly helping the airline cover its fuel tab.

items.title: ServiceNow CEO defends the company's relevancy, touting a kill switch for rogue AI agents
items.description: ServiceNow CEO Bill McDermott said the company is becoming increasingly important as businesses deploy more autonomous AI agents.

items.title: Tesla misses on earnings, as free cash flow turns negative and margins slide
items.description: Tesla's earnings report lands in the midst of a slide in the company's stock price even as its core auto business is rebounding.

items.title: John Paulson says we are in the early stages of a long-term bull market for gold
items.description: He said demand for bullion continues to broaden, led by central banks that have been adding to their reserves alongside growing private-sector interest.

items.title: U.S. signs nuclear power agreement with Saudi Arabia
items.description: The agreement will provide U.S. companies with access to Saudi Arabia's nuclear energy program, the Department of Energy said.

items.title: Analysis: Iran war energy shock hits the U.S. economy as gas and diesel prices climb
items.description: A new asymmetry in the market means fuel prices won't fall quickly even if the shooting in the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz stops.

items.title: Senate panel advances China auto bill that could bar Mercedes-Benz from U.S.
items.description: Mercedes-Benz’s largest individual shareholder is Chinese state-owned BAIC. Cruz warned the bill could hurt Mercedes and accused GM of backing it.

items.title: House advances bill barring sitting Congress members from buying stocks
items.description: The House on Wednesday advanced a bill that would bar members of Congress from purchasing additional stocks while in office.
